title: Context stuffing and lost-in-the-middle
description: Packing many passages into the window wastes tokens and buries relevant ones in the middle where models attend least.
body: Use when deciding how many passages to include. Adding more passages is not strictly better: it costs tokens, risks truncation, and models tend to use information at the start and end of a long context more than the middle, so a relevant passage buried mid-context can be ignored. Do not concatenate every candidate, and avoid placing the best passage in the middle of a long block. Actions: rerank, trim to the most relevant few, deduplicate, and order so key passages are not buried. Validation signals include a test that adding low-relevance passages does not improve and may hurt answer quality. Risk boundary: stuffing that truncates a cited passage breaks grounding; treat it as a failure.
